item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly prefer this version of the GOC. It makes a lot more sense than the original Kill Kill Kill and is a good counter to the Foundation. And as has been touched on in other tales and newer Skips and the like, there's valid reasoning for both organizations to exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did I never see this until now?

As Eugenio's creator, I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My headcanon dictates that while the SCPF handles things that are difficult to understand and might loldestroy the world if let loose (or even messed with) the GOC is more like a paranormal police force that deals with the more 'everyday' paranormal stuff. Vampire or dragon? Ok to shoot/blow up. Endless stairs filled with a scary white face? That's the Foundation's job. I imagine that there is a lot of cross-pollination in missions between th two groups as they hand off stuff to the other. The GOC doesn't want another teleporting chair, but the Foundation doesn't have the resources to box up every last goblin they come across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly how it's being portrayed here, the problem is is that it's usually our Containment approach vs the GOC destroy approach. Just with the way this paints that relationship, it casts some uncertainty and pointlessness on past skips and tales. It also makes the relationship between the GOC and groups of interests it's usually been hostile to, like the Wanderer's Library/The Serpent's Hand kind of a pointless unless they're being reworked a little in this canon as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My personal guess - and this is speaking as both a newbie to the wider Foundation, and to the GOC canon - is that the Foundation has a more research-orientated focus, and that is where its "edge" among the supernatural community comes from. Like, the GOC can complain about the Foundation's unethical treatment of D-class personnel - and those are some really valid points - but at the end of the day they won't have as much data on how the supernatural interacts with baseline human while we do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I've been toying with that idea for a long, long time.

(now that you've reminded me, I might resume progress with it, even)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I'm just a wee greenhorn here, but I had the same problem with this as Lumancer (Mostly his first three contentions). Granted I've only lurked the site for a couple years but this particular story kind of seems out of place in the Foundation universe despite the fact I feel the rest of the GoC case files fit pretty well. It just makes the Foundation seem unnecessary, and makes the antagonism between the Serpent's Hand/Wanderer's Library people and the GoC seem somewhat pointless with how tolerant and accepting the GoC seemingly is to the supernatural.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit Clef, stop being reasonable so I can complain properly :<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most confusing to me, the very existence of new GOC as a fairly paranormal-tolerant UN-backed organization calls into question why the SCPF is permitted to exist (independently) at all. I may be stretching here, but if they're willing to seek diplomatic relations with Eugenio of all things then the Foundations is arguably guilty of war crimes at minimum- and much worse when shit like Tophet or Montauk is brought into consideration.

"Gentlemen. We have an organization here that is guilty of war crimes and crimes against humanity. They must be stopped."

"Where are they located?"

"All over the world, but mostly ███████."

"Damn. That's a Security Council nation. Make an official request through the proper channels?"

"We tried. The ambassador said, probably quite truthfully, that he does not know what we are talking about."

"I guess that rules out trying to get the international community to put pressure on them. It's not like we can tell the world governments why we should sanction ███████ because they're harboring a known human rights violator. Can we send a STRIKE team? Do this quietly?"

"Second Mission concerns. The organization is too deeply entrenched. First Mission concerns besides… despite what they're doing, they're maintaining containment of these parathreats. I can't guarantee that we can do the same. Especially during the strike. Too risky, we could lose control of the parathreat and end up in an EES."

"Stupid Eschatological Event Sequences. All right. Keep monitoring them. Make a list of their human rights violations. Our hands are tied for now, but we'll keep this in mind for the day we go Pizzicato."

"I'll add it to the big list."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now, to be fair, the O5 Council turning out to be the Olympian gods would be badass as fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this was meant to be a comic interlude, but something about the piece has really solidified the reasons why this whole vision of the GOC has been bugging me. There's an increasingly large disparity between the content that formed my first impressions of the GOC and the group that appears in these tales. I could cite specific articles and such, but what it all boils down to is that we've collectively pointed to the GOC for the "destroy, destroy, destroy" mindset- and as a foil to make the Foundation seem reasonable by comparison- for a very long time. This version of the GOC (hereafter "new GOC") is a flagrant contradiction of that entire notion:

  • The SCPF is clearly less reasonable than new GOC, though a big part of this appears to be a much friendlier supernatural universe than that implied by the mainlist.
  • There's no logical reason for the Wanderer's Library types to refer to new GOC as "The Bookburners" when employment opportunities are ostensibly at least as likely as extermination.
  • Conversely, given how widely magic seems to be used by new GOC, I'm having a hard time justifying the notion that the SCPF is willing to work with them at all.
  • Most confusing to me, the very existence of new GOC as a fairly paranormal-tolerant UN-backed organization calls into question why the SCPF is permitted to exist (independently) at all. I may be stretching here, but if they're willing to seek diplomatic relations with Eugenio of all things then the Foundations is arguably guilty of war crimes at minimum- and much worse when shit like Tophet or Montauk is brought into consideration.

I know, I know, "there is no canon", but this is starting to stick in my craw as badly as… I dunno, the entire O5 counsel turning out to be the Olympian gods or something. In isolation, new GOC is pretty cool, and in the Unfounded canon in particular the slightly more reasonable riff on their old selves made for some really awesome world building. But outside of that canon, the very fact that they are the GOC and not some other group clashes really strangely with a lot of precedent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would've upvoted anyway, but you earned my vote simply for the mention of Carolingian minuscule. As an amateur calligrapher, such details make me happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm giving this my official author seal of canon approval.

I am now editing the original article to include this partial backstory.
